name: File Descriptor Limit Triage
summary: Collect read-only evidence for too-many-open-files failures.
description: This skill helps HPC users and support teams diagnose EMFILE, too many open files, file descriptor pressure, and nofile limit mismatches in batch jobs, workflow engines, data loaders, MPI runs, and many-small-file workloads without changing process or system limits.

tools.name: ulimit
tools.notes: Shell builtin used to report the current process nofile limit.

tools.name: lsof
tools.notes: Useful when permitted for per-process open file summaries.

inputs.name: pid
inputs.description: Optional process id for a running job step, worker, workflow engine, or data loader to inspect.

inputs.name: log_file
inputs.description: Optional job or application log with too-many-open-files symptoms.

outputs.name: file_descriptor_triage_report
outputs.description: Read-only report with shell nofile limits, optional process limits, open fd counts, optional lsof summary, and log clues.

outputs.name: review_checklist
outputs.description: Checklist for deciding whether the issue is per-process limits, worker fan-out, many small files, leaked descriptors, or workflow-engine staging.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and example artifacts.
Collect a read-only report for the current shell process.
Run against a user-owned process or job log that shows EMFILE and confirm no limits, process state, files, or scheduler state are changed.
